# WebWaka OS — Verticals Execution Prompts: Transport Extended

**Document type:** Agent execution prompt set
**Scope:** Transport extended verticals — 8 verticals covered
**Repo:** https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os
**Branch base:** `main`
**Milestone targets:** M9–M12
**Status:** Ready for agent execution — all P1 Transport verticals complete (M8c)
**Generated by:** MASTER_CONTINUATION_PROMPT.md — Set D

---

> **3-in-1 Platform Note:**
> Transport verticals primarily serve **Pillar 1 (Ops)** and **Pillar 3 (Marketplace/Discovery)**.
> Some logistics-facing verticals also activate **Pillar 2 (Branding)** for corporate service profiles.
> **SuperAgent AI is cross-cutting — NOT a fourth pillar.** AI capabilities: `ROUTE_OPTIMIZATION`, `FLEET_EFFICIENCY_REPORT`, `DELIVERY_ETA_PREDICTION` — L2 max autonomy.
> Political content autonomy cap does not apply here; transport AI at L2 standard cap.
> See `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md` for the full pillar map.

### General rules for all agents using these prompts

- Never make assumptions about WebWaka's architecture. Always read the referenced documents and code first.
- Research deeply — especially FRSC, NCS, NIMASA, and NAGAFF regulatory requirements before implementing any route or transition guard.
- Every monetary value must be an integer in kobo (P9). No decimals, no floats.
- All volumes (cargo weight, fuel, water) must be stored as integers (kg, ml, litres × 1000 etc.) — no floats.
- No cross-tenant data leakage (T3). Every D1 query scopes to `tenant_id`.
- SuperAgent AI: all AI features route through `packages/superagent`. USSD excluded (P12). NDPR consent required (P10).

---

## TASK V-TRN-EXT-1: Clearing & Forwarding Agent Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `clearing-agent`
- **Priority:** P2-Top100 — Nigeria handles 70% of West Africa's sea trade; Apapa, Tin Can Island, Onne, Calabar ports have thousands of licensed clearing agents; NAGAFF and NCS regulation
- **Milestone target:** M9
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 2 (Branding) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","branding","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:**
  - Verticals master plan: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md
  - 3-in-1 platform architecture: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md
  - Verticals FSM engine: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/packages/verticals/src/
  - Platform invariants: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/platform-invariants.md
  - Entitlement model: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/entitlement-model.md
  - AI integration framework: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md
  - SuperAgent product spec: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/superagent/02-product-spec.md

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in customs brokerage and freight forwarding management software, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian customs brokerage — NCS (Nigeria Customs Service) licensed agent (CAC-06 licence), NAGAFF (National Association of Government Approved Freight Forwarders) membership, Apapa/Tin Can port workflows, customs duty payment (DUTipay), NAFDAC import permit for food/medicine
- Shipment tracking, customs entry filing reference, duty assessment in kobo, container management
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode; WebWaka SuperAgent integration; Verticals FSM engine

---

**1. Mandatory context reading (100% before execution):**

- `docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md` — entry for `clearing-agent`
- `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md`
- `docs/governance/platform-invariants.md` — P2, P9, P10, P12, P13, T3, T4
- `infra/db/seeds/0004_verticals-master.csv` — row for `clearing-agent`
- `packages/verticals/src/` — FSM engine; `packages/auth/src/guards.ts`
- `docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md`; `infra/db/migrations/` — next migration number

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks:
- Nigerian customs clearing market — NBS trade data, Apapa port daily container volume, average clearing timeframe (3–21 days), major client types (importers, manufacturers, NGOs)
- Competitors: SingleWindow.ng (government), TradePort (software), Excel-based agent management
- Key features: shipment register (vessel, bill of lading, container number, client, cargo description, declared_value_kobo), customs duty assessment (duty_amount_kobo, VAT_kobo, levies_kobo), Form M tracking (CBN forex import approval), NAFDAC import permit reference, port charges in kobo, clearance status tracking, agent fee invoice (professional_fee_kobo)

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give Nigerian clearing agents a digital shipment register, customs duty tracker, Form M pipeline, and client invoice management — all in kobo
- **Schema:**
  - `clearing_agent_profiles` — company_name, NCS_licence, NAGAFF_number, CAC_RC, workspace_id, tenant_id, status
  - `clearing_shipments` — client_phone, vessel_name, bill_of_lading, container_number, cargo_description, declared_value_kobo, duty_amount_kobo, vat_kobo, port_charges_kobo, professional_fee_kobo, form_m_number, nafdac_permit_ref, port (apapa/tin_can/onne/calabar/other), status (lodgement/examination/duty_assessment/duty_paid/released/delivered), tenant_id
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `ncs_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `FLEET_EFFICIENCY_REPORT` (shipment clearance time benchmark) — L2; Pro plan (given high-value transactions)
- **Regulatory hook:** NCS licence + NAGAFF number at `ncs_verified`
- **KYC:** Tier 3 — clearing agents handle FX-denominated shipment values; full CBN KYC required

---

**3. Implementation workflow:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-clearing-agent` from `main`.

**3a. Schema:** `[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_clearing_agent.sql`. All monetary in kobo (P9). `tenant_id NOT NULL` (T3). Index on `status`, `tenant_id`.

**3b. Package:** `packages/verticals-clearing-agent/src/index.ts` — `registerClearingAgentVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`. AI: aggregate shipment counts and clearance times by port — no client details, bill of lading, or container numbers (P13). L2 autonomy cap.

**3c. Routes:** `apps/api/src/routes/verticals/clearing-agent.ts`. Full CRUD for shipments, status transitions. AI blocked on USSD (P12).

**3d. T3:** All D1 queries scoped to `tenant_id`.

---

**4. QA and verification:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-clearing-agent/src/clearing-agent.test.ts`

Positive: register; FSM to `ncs_verified` with NCS licence + NAGAFF; shipment with integer declared_value_kobo, duty_amount_kobo, professional_fee_kobo (P9); status progression through clearance stages; AI benchmark with aiRights + NDPR. Negative: fractional kobo; cross-tenant → 403 (T3); AI triply blocked; `ncs_verified` without licence → typed error; invalid FSM → typed error (T4). Security: unauth → 401; Tier 3 KYC → 403 for lower tiers.

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Finalize and push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/clearing-agent): Clearing Agent vertical — shipment register, customs duty, Form M pipeline (M9)"
git push origin feat/vertical-clearing-agent
```
PR checklist: FSM, migration, `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`, labeled `3in1:pillar-1/2/3`, Tier 3 KYC, SuperAgent L2, T3, ≥15 tests, zero typecheck/lint errors.

---

## TASK V-TRN-EXT-2: Courier Service Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `courier`
- **Priority:** P2-Top100 — E-commerce growth is driving massive demand for last-mile courier services; GIG Logistics, DHL, Speedaf, Red Star Express, and thousands of SME couriers
- **Milestone target:** M9
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 2 (Branding) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","branding","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:** (standard links as above)

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in courier and last-mile delivery management software, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian courier and logistics sector — GIG Logistics, DHL Nigeria, Speedaf, ACE Express, Red Star Express; NCC e-commerce delivery compliance, NAFDAC for pharmaceutical courier, NPC for data collection
- Parcel tracking, rider network management, COD (cash-on-delivery) reconciliation in kobo, hub-to-hub routing
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode; WebWaka SuperAgent integration; Verticals FSM engine

---

**1. Mandatory context reading (100% before execution):**

All standard documents — `verticals-master-plan.md`, `3in1-platform-architecture.md`, `platform-invariants.md`, seed CSV, FSM engine, guards, AI framework, next migration number.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks:
- Nigerian courier market — NBS logistics sector data, GIG Logistics rider network size, average delivery cost per km in naira, COD transaction value, e-commerce parcel volume (Jumia, Konga, social commerce sellers)
- Competitors: GIG Go app, Sendbox, Terminal Africa, Kwik Delivery
- Key features: parcel intake (sender phone, receiver phone, weight_grams INTEGER, parcel description, pickup_address, delivery_address, COD_amount_kobo, delivery_fee_kobo, tracking_code), delivery status (received/picked_up/in_transit/out_for_delivery/delivered/returned), rider assignment, COD collection and remittance, hub management

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give Nigerian courier SMEs a digital parcel management system — intake, tracking, rider dispatch, COD reconciliation, and AI delivery performance reporting
- **Schema:**
  - `courier_profiles` — company_name, NCC_registered, CAC_RC, workspace_id, tenant_id, status
  - `courier_parcels` — tracking_code TEXT, sender_phone, receiver_phone, weight_grams INTEGER, description, pickup_address, delivery_address, delivery_fee_kobo, cod_amount_kobo, rider_id, status (intake/picked_up/in_transit/out_for_delivery/delivered/returned/failed), tenant_id
  - `courier_riders` — rider_name, phone, vehicle_type (bicycle/motorcycle/van), license_number, status (available/on_delivery/offline), tenant_id
  - `courier_cod_remittances` — parcel_id, collected_kobo, remitted_kobo, remittance_date, tenant_id
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `cac_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `DELIVERY_ETA_PREDICTION` (route + delivery time analysis), `FLEET_EFFICIENCY_REPORT` — L2; Growth plan
- **Regulatory hook:** CAC RC at `cac_verified`; NCC e-commerce compliance flag
- **KYC:** Tier 2 for COD remittance operations; Tier 3 for pharmaceutical courier (NAFDAC)

---

**3. Implementation workflow:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-courier` from `main`.

**3a. Schema:** `[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_courier.sql`. Weight in integer grams. All monetary in kobo (P9). `tenant_id NOT NULL` (T3).

**3b. Package:** `packages/verticals-courier/src/index.ts` — `registerCourierVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`. AI: aggregate delivery counts, average times, and revenue by zone — no sender/receiver phone, parcel descriptions (P13). L2 cap.

**3c. Routes:** Full CRUD for parcels, riders, COD remittances. AI blocked on USSD (P12).

**3d. T3:** All queries scoped.

---

**4. QA and verification:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-courier/src/courier.test.ts`

Positive: register; FSM to `cac_verified`; parcel with integer weight_grams, delivery_fee_kobo, cod_amount_kobo (P9); parcel status progression; rider assignment; COD remittance with integer collected_kobo; AI delivery report with aiRights + NDPR. Negative: float weight rejected; fractional kobo; cross-tenant → 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM → typed error. Security: unauth → 401; KYC → 403.

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Finalize and push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/courier): Courier Service vertical — parcel tracking, rider dispatch, COD reconciliation (M9)"
git push origin feat/vertical-courier
```
PR checklist: FSM, migration, `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`, labeled, KYC guards, SuperAgent L2, T3, ≥15 tests, zero errors.

## TASK V-TRN-EXT-3: Dispatch Rider Network Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `dispatch-rider`
- **Priority:** P2-Top100 — Intra-city dispatch is one of the most in-demand logistics categories in Lagos, Abuja, Port Harcourt; FRSC and VIO rider licence verification critical
- **Milestone target:** M9
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:** (standard links)

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in on-demand dispatch and gig rider management, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian dispatch rider sector — FRSC motorcycle licence verification, VIO (Vehicle Inspection Officers) roadworthiness for commercial motorcycles, Lagos dispatch aggregators (Gokada survived as B2B, Kwik, Bolt courier), rider onboarding
- Rider commission management (percentage per delivery), live dispatch job assignment, COD handling in kobo
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode; WebWaka SuperAgent integration; Verticals FSM engine

---

**1. Mandatory context reading (100% before execution):**

All standard documents plus: `packages/verticals-rideshare/` for reference on rider FSM pattern already implemented in M8c.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks:
- Nigerian dispatch rider market — Kwik Delivery (1,000+ riders), Gokada B2B, Bolt courier, intra-city parcel volume; average dispatch fee (₦500–₦3,000 per job); Lagos motorcycle ban implications and workarounds
- Key features: rider roster (name, FRSC licence, VIO cert, vehicle plate, commission_pct), dispatch job creation (pickup, dropoff, package description, fee_kobo), job assignment to rider, COD collection, rider earnings log, daily performance dashboard

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give Nigerian dispatch businesses a digital rider roster, job dispatch system, COD reconciliation, and AI fleet efficiency report
- **Schema:**
  - `dispatch_rider_profiles` — company_name, CAC_RC, workspace_id, tenant_id, status
  - `dispatch_riders` — rider_name, phone, FRSC_licence, VIO_cert, vehicle_plate, commission_pct INTEGER, status (active/suspended/offline), tenant_id
  - `dispatch_jobs` — pickup_address, dropoff_address, package_description, fee_kobo, cod_amount_kobo, rider_id, status (created/assigned/picked_up/delivered/cod_collected), customer_phone, tenant_id
  - `rider_earnings` — rider_id, job_id, gross_fee_kobo, commission_kobo, net_payout_kobo, payout_date, tenant_id
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `frsc_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `FLEET_EFFICIENCY_REPORT` (rider performance, delivery success rate) — L2; Growth plan
- **Regulatory hook:** CAC RC at `claimed`; FRSC verification gate at `frsc_verified`
- **KYC:** Tier 2 for rider payout operations; Tier 1 for basic job dispatch

---

**3. Implementation:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-dispatch-rider`. Schema: `[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_dispatch_rider.sql`. Commission as integer percentage (0–100). All monetary in kobo (P9). Tenant scoped (T3). Package: `registerDispatchRiderVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`. AI: aggregate job counts and delivery success rate — no rider names, plates, FRSC numbers (P13). USSD excluded (P12).

---

**4. QA:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-dispatch-rider/src/dispatch-rider.test.ts`

Positive: register; FSM to `frsc_verified` with valid FRSC + VIO; job with integer fee_kobo, cod_amount_kobo; job status progression; rider earnings with integer commission_kobo, net_payout_kobo; AI fleet report with aiRights + NDPR. Negative: fractional kobo; cross-tenant → 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM → typed error; `frsc_verified` without FRSC licence → typed error. Security: unauth → 401; KYC → 403.

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/dispatch-rider): Dispatch Rider vertical — job dispatch, rider earnings, FRSC compliance (M9)"
git push origin feat/vertical-dispatch-rider
```
PR checklist: FSM, migration, `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`, labeled, FRSC gate tested, SuperAgent L2, T3, ≥15 tests, zero errors.

## TASK V-TRN-EXT-4: Airport Shuttle Service Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `airport-shuttle`

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in airport ground transportation management, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:** Nigerian airport shuttle market — FAAN licensed operators, FRSC commercial vehicle compliance, airport pick-up zone allocation, flight tracking integration readiness (no direct API initially — customer provides flight time), advance booking management in kobo.

---

**1. Mandatory context reading:** All standard documents.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks: airport transfer market in Lagos/Abuja — average price per trip, FAAN permit requirements, competitors (Uber Black, private drivers, hotel shuttles, Taxify corporate); key features: booking (passenger phone, flight number, pickup_time, destination, vehicle_type, fare_kobo, status), driver assignment, advance booking calendar, FAAN permit tracking.

Execution plan:
- **Schema:** `airport_shuttle_profiles` (company_name, FAAN_permit, FRSC_commercial_licence, CAC_RC, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `shuttle_bookings` (passenger_phone, flight_number, pickup_airport, destination, pickup_time INTEGER, driver_id, vehicle_id, fare_kobo, status (booked/confirmed/dispatched/completed/cancelled), tenant_id); `shuttle_vehicles` (vehicle_plate, type (sedan/SUV/minibus), capacity, driver_id, frsc_cert, tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `faan_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `ROUTE_OPTIMIZATION` (airport-to-destination time estimation), `FLEET_EFFICIENCY_REPORT` — L2; Growth plan
- **KYC:** Tier 2 for operations; Tier 3 for corporate accounts

---

**3. Implementation:** Branch `feat/vertical-airport-shuttle`. `registerAirportShuttleVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`. Kobo (P9). Tenant scoped (T3). AI: aggregate booking counts and route revenue — no passenger phone/flight number (P13). USSD excluded (P12).

---

**4. QA:** Register; FSM to `faan_verified`; booking with integer fare_kobo; status progression; vehicle assignment; AI route report with aiRights + NDPR; fractional kobo rejected; cross-tenant 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM typed error. Unauth 401; KYC 403. **Minimum: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Push:** `git commit -m "feat(vertical/airport-shuttle): Airport Shuttle vertical — bookings, FAAN compliance, AI route optimization (M12)"`. PR checklist standard.

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in road freight fleet management, working on WebWaka OS.

- **Schema:** `cargo_truck_profiles` (company_name, CAC_or_coop_number, FRSC_operator_licence, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `cargo_trucks` (plate, make, model, tonnage_kg INTEGER, frsc_cert_expiry INTEGER, status (available/on_trip/maintenance), tenant_id); `cargo_trips` (truck_id, origin, destination, cargo_description, cargo_weight_kg INTEGER, hire_rate_kobo, client_phone, departure_date INTEGER, arrival_date INTEGER, status (loading/in_transit/delivered/paid), tenant_id); `truck_expenses` (truck_id, expense_type (fuel/maintenance/levy/toll), amount_kobo, expense_date, tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `frsc_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `FLEET_EFFICIENCY_REPORT` (trip revenue per km, fuel efficiency), `ROUTE_OPTIMIZATION` — L2; Growth plan
- **KYC:** Tier 2 for trip hire invoicing; Tier 3 for fleet financing products

---

---

**4. QA:** Register; FSM to `frsc_verified`; truck with integer tonnage_kg; trip with integer cargo_weight_kg, hire_rate_kobo; expense with integer amount_kobo; AI fleet report with aiRights + NDPR; float weight rejected; fractional kobo rejected; cross-tenant 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM typed error. Unauth 401; KYC 403. **Minimum: 15 tests.**

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in port logistics and container depot management, working on WebWaka OS.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Key features: container intake (container number, type 20/40 TEU, weight_kg INTEGER, client, status (stuffing/stripping/storage/released)), storage duration billing (daily_rate_kobo × days = storage_charge_kobo), crane operation log, NCS customs release confirmation, demurrage reference.

Execution plan:
- **Schema:** `container_depot_profiles` (company_name, NCS_licence, NPA_licence, CAC_RC, depot_location, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `container_records` (container_number TEXT, container_type (20ft/40ft), weight_kg INTEGER, client_phone, operation_type (stuffing/stripping/storage), daily_storage_rate_kobo, days_in_depot INTEGER, storage_charge_kobo, ncs_release_number, status (received/in_operation/awaiting_release/released), tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `ncs_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `FLEET_EFFICIENCY_REPORT` (container throughput analysis) — L2; Pro plan
- **KYC:** Tier 3 — high-value port operations; FX-related charges

---

## TASK V-TRN-EXT-7: Ferry / Water Transport Operator Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `ferry`
- **Priority:** P3-Specialist — Water transport across Lagos Lagoon, Niger Delta waterways, and Cross River is significant; NIMASA (Nigerian Maritime Administration and Safety Agency) regulates operators
- **Milestone target:** M12
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:** (standard links)

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in maritime and water transport management, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:** Nigerian water transport — Lagos Lagoon ferry routes (Ikorodu-Lagos Island, Badagry), Niger Delta speedboats (Warri, Asaba, Port Harcourt); NIMASA vessel registration, NRC (river crossing compliance), passenger manifest, ticket pricing in kobo, vessel maintenance log.

---

**1. Mandatory context reading:** All standard documents.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Key features: vessel register (name, type, capacity_passengers INTEGER, NIMASA_reg, route), trip log (vessel_id, route, departure_time, passenger_count INTEGER, ticket_price_kobo, total_revenue_kobo, status), passenger manifest (trip_id, manifest_count INTEGER, tenant_id), maintenance log.

Execution plan:
- **Schema:** `ferry_operator_profiles` (company_name, NIMASA_licence, NRC_compliance, CAC_RC, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `ferry_vessels` (vessel_name, type (speedboat/ferry/boat), capacity_passengers INTEGER, nimasa_reg, route_description, status (operational/maintenance/decommissioned), tenant_id); `ferry_trips` (vessel_id, route, departure_time INTEGER, arrival_time INTEGER, passenger_count INTEGER, ticket_price_kobo, total_revenue_kobo, status (scheduled/departed/arrived/cancelled), tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `nimasa_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `ROUTE_OPTIMIZATION` (passenger flow per route), `FLEET_EFFICIENCY_REPORT` — L2; Growth plan
- **KYC:** Tier 2 for revenue operations; Tier 3 for cargo ferry operations

---

**3. Implementation:** Branch `feat/vertical-ferry`. `registerFerryVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`. Capacity and passenger counts as integers. Prices in kobo (P9). Tenant scoped (T3). AI: aggregate trip counts, passenger volumes, and revenue by route — no individual passenger data (P13). USSD excluded (P12).

---

**4. QA:** Register; FSM to `nimasa_verified`; vessel with integer capacity; trip with integer passenger_count, ticket_price_kobo, total_revenue_kobo; AI route report with aiRights + NDPR; fractional kobo rejected; float capacity rejected; cross-tenant 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM typed error. Unauth 401; KYC 403. **Minimum: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Push:** `git commit -m "feat(vertical/ferry): Ferry Operator vertical — vessel management, trip logs, NIMASA compliance (M12)"`. PR checklist standard.

---

## TASK V-TRN-EXT-8: Road Transport Workers Union (NURTW) Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `nurtw`
- **Priority:** P3-Specialist — NURTW and RTEAN are powerful transport unions across Nigeria; manage motor park dues, member welfare, and route allocations; politically sensitive (L3 HITL for any AI output referencing membership or leadership)
- **Milestone target:** M12
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:** (standard links)

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in trade union management software, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:** Nigerian road transport union structure — NURTW (National Union of Road Transport Workers), RTEAN (Road Transport Employers Association of Nigeria), state and local chapter management, member dues collection in kobo, park space allocation, welfare fund management; political content AI cap applies (L3 HITL).

---

**1. Mandatory context reading:** All standard documents. Additional critical reads:
- `docs/governance/ai-capability-matrix.md` — HITL requirements for civic/political content
- `docs/governance/superagent/02-product-spec.md` — L3 HITL enforcement for sensitive content

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks: NURTW structure — national body, state executives, park chairman level, member count per state, dues collection model (daily levy per vehicle, annual membership fee); key pain points: cash leakage in dues collection, no digital member register, no welfare claim tracking; competitors: none; features: member register (driver name, vehicle plate, vehicle type, member_since, dues_status), daily dues collection log (member_id, date, amount_kobo, collector_id), welfare claim log (member_id, claim_type, amount_kobo, status), park space allocation, route permit management.

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give NURTW/RTEAN state and park chapters a digital member register, dues collection log, welfare claims tracker, and route permit management — all in kobo; AI outputs about membership or leadership require L3 HITL
- **Schema:**
  - `nurtw_profiles` — union_name, chapter_level (national/state/lga/park), NURTW_registration, state, workspace_id, tenant_id, status
  - `union_members` — member_name, vehicle_plate, vehicle_type, member_since INTEGER, monthly_dues_kobo, dues_status (current/arrears), tenant_id
  - `union_dues_log` — member_id, collection_date INTEGER, amount_kobo, collector_id, tenant_id
  - `union_welfare_claims` — member_id, claim_type (accident/medical/death_benefit), amount_kobo, status (submitted/approved/paid/rejected), tenant_id
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `nurtw_registered` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `MEMBER_ENGAGEMENT_REPORT` — **L3 HITL MANDATORY** (political/civic content autonomy cap); any AI output about union membership or leadership must not be published without human review; NDPR consent required; USSD excluded (P12)
- **Regulatory hook:** NURTW registration number at `nurtw_registered`
- **KYC:** Tier 1 for basic dues collection; Tier 2 for welfare payout operations

---

**3. Implementation:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-nurtw`. `registerNurtwVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`. All monetary in kobo (P9). Tenant scoped (T3). AI: aggregate dues revenue and member count — no member names, vehicle plates (P13). **L3 HITL flag must be set on all AI advisory calls** for this vertical. USSD excluded (P12).

---

**4. QA:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-nurtw/src/nurtw.test.ts`

Positive: register; FSM to `nurtw_registered`; member with integer monthly_dues_kobo; dues log with integer amount_kobo; welfare claim with integer amount_kobo; AI member report returns with HITL flag set + aiRights + NDPR. Negative: fractional kobo; cross-tenant → 403; AI with aiRights false → blocked; NDPR absent → blocked; USSD session → blocked; AI missing HITL flag → blocked (test that L3 HITL is enforced); invalid FSM → typed error. Security: unauth → 401; KYC → 403.

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

**CRITICAL TEST:** Verify that the AI advisory route sets `autonomy_level: "L3_HITL"` on the SuperAgent call and that output is not auto-published.

---

**5. Finalize and push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/nurtw): NURTW Union vertical — member register, dues collection, welfare claims, L3 HITL AI (M12)"
git push origin feat/vertical-nurtw
```

- [ ] Vertical registered in FSM

- [ ] PR labeled: `3in1:pillar-1`, `3in1:pillar-3`
- [ ] L3 HITL flag set and tested on all AI advisory routes
- [ ] Member names and vehicle plates explicitly excluded from AI calls (P13)
- [ ] USSD exclusion enforced (P12)

- [ ] Tests ≥ 15, all passing including HITL enforcement test
- [ ] Zero typecheck errors (`pnpm -r typecheck`)
- [ ] Zero lint errors (`pnpm -r lint`)
